source_organisation: United States Census Bureau, Population Estimates Program (PEP)
cleaning_or_aggregation: Filtered to state-level rows (SUMLEV 40); extracted POPESTIMATE2011 to POPESTIMATE2014 and reshaped to State-Year long format; restricted to the 49 states present in the Superstore US data.
why_it_complements_superstore: Provides a market-size denominator so sales can be assessed per capita rather than only in absolute terms.
limitation: Population is an annual state total and does not capture within-state demand variation or individual customer behaviour.

source_organisation: United States Census Bureau, Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates (SAIPE) Program
cleaning_or_aggregation: Downloaded the SAIPE state-and-county file for each year 2011-2014; kept state-total rows (county FIPS 000); extracted the Median Household Income column.
why_it_complements_superstore: Approximates household purchasing power, letting sales and margins be read against the economic capacity of each state.
limitation: SAIPE income is a modelled estimate for the whole state population, not specifically Superstore's customers.

source_organisation: United States Bureau of Labor Statistics, Local Area Unemployment Statistics (LAUS)
cleaning_or_aggregation: Retrieved statewide monthly unemployment-rate series via the BLS public API; computed each year's annual rate as the mean of its twelve monthly values.
why_it_complements_superstore: Captures local economic stress, allowing sales/margin/discount patterns to be compared against the post-recession labour-market recovery of 2011-2014.
limitation: Unemployment is a state aggregate and a coincident indicator; it contextualises rather than explains individual order outcomes.
